UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile in the Health Tracker application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

ActivityData.title: ActivityData
ActivityData.description: Stores activity data for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActivityData entity.

steps.description: Number of steps taken by the user.

distance.description: Distance covered by the user in kilometers.

activityLevel.description: A percentage representing the user's activity level.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the activity data was recorded.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeoFence entity.

radius.description: Radius of the geo-fence in meters.

EmergencyContact.title: EmergencyContact
EmergencyContact.description: Represents an emergency contact for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmergencyContact entity.

phoneNumber.description: Phone number of the emergency contact.

relationship.description: Relationship to the user (e.g., parent, spouse).

AiHealthState.title: AiHealthState
AiHealthState.description: Stores the AI Chatbot short term memory for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AiHealthState entity.

state.description: The AI Chatbot memory for the user health state.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of when the state was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alert entity.

type.description: Type of alert (e.g., geo-fence breach, abnormal heart rate, SOS).

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the alert was triggered.

acknowledged.description: Indicates if the alert has been acknowledged.

Itinerary.title: Itinerary
Itinerary.description: Represents a scheduled event or activity for a user.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. This collection uses path-based ownership. The document ID is always 'data'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores activity data for a user. This collection uses path-based ownership. No denormalization required.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: activityDataId
params.description: The unique identifier for the activity data entry.

definition.description: Stores geo-fence data for a user. This collection uses path-based ownership. No denormalization required.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: geoFenceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the geo-fence.

definition.description: Stores emergency contact information for a user. This collection uses path-based ownership. No denormalization required.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: emergencyContactId
params.description: The unique identifier for the emergency contact.

definition.description: Stores the AI health state for a user. This collection uses path-based ownership. The document ID is always 'data'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores alerts for a user. This collection uses path-based ownership. No denormalization required.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier for the alert.

definition.description: Stores itinerary/schedule items for a user. This collection uses path-based ownership. No denormalization required.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: itineraryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the itinerary item.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). It is designed to support the Health Tracker application's features, including user profiles, activity data, geo-fencing, emergency alerts, and an AI chatbot. Each collection is designed with homogeneous security requirements in mind. Path-based ownership is used to secure user-specific data, enabling simple and efficient security rules. The structure avoids `get()` calls in security rules, thus enabling atomic operations (transactions/batches).
